title: Cesena

infobox.value: [St. John the Baptist](/wiki/St._John_the_Baptist "St. John the Baptist")

(Italian: [[tʃeˈzɛːna]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"); [Romagnol](/wiki/Romagnol_language "Romagnol language"): *Cisêna*) is a city and *[comune](/wiki/Comune "Comune")* (municipality) in the [Emilia-Romagna](/wiki/Emilia-Romagna "Emilia-Romagna") region of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"); and - with [Forlì](/wiki/Forl%C3%AC "Forlì") - is the capital of the [Province of Forlì-Cesena](/wiki/Province_of_Forl%C3%AC-Cesena "Province of Forlì-Cesena"). Served by [Autostrada A14](/wiki/Autostrada_A14_(Italy) "Autostrada A14 (Italy)"), and located near the [Apennine Mountains](/wiki/Apennine_Mountains "Apennine Mountains"), about 15 kilometres (9 miles) from the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea"). The total population is 97,137.

**Cesena** (Italian: [[tʃeˈzɛːna]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Italian "Help:IPA/Italian"); [Romagnol](/wiki/Romagnol_language "Romagnol language"): *Cisêna*) is a city and *[comune](/wiki/Comune "Comune")* (municipality) in the [Emilia-Romagna](/wiki/Emilia-Romagna "Emilia-Romagna") region of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"); and - with [Forlì](/wiki/Forl%C3%AC "Forlì") - is the capital of the [Province of Forlì-Cesena](/wiki/Province_of_Forl%C3%AC-Cesena "Province of Forlì-Cesena"). Served by [Autostrada A14](/wiki/Autostrada_A14_(Italy) "Autostrada A14 (Italy)"), and located near the [Apennine Mountains](/wiki/Apennine_Mountains "Apennine Mountains"), about 15 kilometres (9 miles) from the [Adriatic Sea](/wiki/Adriatic_Sea "Adriatic Sea"). The total population is 97,137.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cesena&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/93/Stemma_del_Comune_di_Cesena_su_Palazzo_Albornoz.jpg/250px-Stemma_del_Comune_di_Cesena_su_Palazzo_Albornoz.jpg)](/wiki/File:Stemma_del_Comune_di_Cesena_su_Palazzo_Albornoz.jpg)

Coat of arms of Cesena on the façade of Palazzo Albornoz, the city hall

Cesena was originally an [Umbrian](/wiki/Umbri "Umbri") or [Etruscan](/wiki/Etruscan_civilization "Etruscan civilization") town, later known as Caesena. After a brief spell under [Gaulish](/wiki/Gaul "Gaul") rule, it was taken over by [Romans](/wiki/Roman_Republic "Roman Republic") in the 3rd century BC. It was a garrison town of strategic importance which was destroyed in the wars between [Gaius Marius](/wiki/Gaius_Marius "Gaius Marius") and [Sulla](/wiki/Lucius_Cornelius_Sulla "Lucius Cornelius Sulla"). [Pliny](/wiki/Pliny_the_Elder "Pliny the Elder") mentions the [wines](/wiki/Wine "Wine") of Cesena as among the best.

Cesena was on the border that the [Exarchate of Ravenna](/wiki/Exarchate_of_Ravenna "Exarchate of Ravenna") shared with the [Lombards](/wiki/Lombards "Lombards"). It was presented to the Papacy by its Frankish conqueror in 754 ([Donation of Pepin](/wiki/Donation_of_Pepin "Donation of Pepin")) and passed back and forth between the popes and the archbishops of Ravenna; it was also briefly a communal republic (1183–1198). It was then long contested between popes and [Holy Roman Emperors](/wiki/Holy_Roman_Emperor "Holy Roman Emperor"). The brief rule by the Forlivese [Ordelaffi](/wiki/Ordelaffi "Ordelaffi") was crushed in 1357 by Papal troops led by Cardinal [Gil de Albornoz](/wiki/Gil_%C3%81lvarez_Carrillo_de_Albornoz "Gil Álvarez Carrillo de Albornoz"), after a long siege heroically endured by Cia degli Ordelaffi, wife of the Lord of [Forlì](/wiki/Forl%C3%AC "Forlì").

The little *[comune](/wiki/Comune "Comune")* revolted again in 1377 during the [War of the Eight Saints](/wiki/War_of_the_Eight_Saints "War of the Eight Saints"). This time it was recaptured by [Breton](/wiki/Breton_people "Breton people") troops of Giovanni Acuto (the English-born [condottiere](/wiki/Condottiere "Condottiere") [John Hawkwood](/wiki/John_Hawkwood "John Hawkwood")) under the command of [Robert, Cardinal of Geneva](/wiki/Antipope_Clement_VII "Antipope Clement VII") (later antipope Clement VII). The latter, acting as the [legate](/wiki/Papal_legate "Papal legate") of [Pope Gregory XI](/wiki/Pope_Gregory_XI "Pope Gregory XI"), directed the savage murder of between 2,500 and 5,000 civilians.[[3]](#cite_note-3) By the laws of war at the time, this was regarded as an atrocity, called the "Cesena Bloodbath", and Cardinal Robert was dubbed the "butcher of Cesena". The following year what remained of Cesena was assigned by the new [Pope Urban VII](/wiki/Pope_Urban_VII "Pope Urban VII") to [Galeotto I Malatesta](/wiki/Galeotto_I_Malatesta "Galeotto I Malatesta").

During the period 1379–1465, the city recovered and prospered under the Malatesta, who rebuilt the [castle](/wiki/Castle "Castle") (called [Rocca Malatestiana](/wiki/Rocca_Malatestiana "Rocca Malatestiana")) overlooking the town. The [Malatestiana Library](/wiki/Malatestiana_Library "Malatestiana Library"), built by near the castle by [Malatesta Novello](/wiki/Malatesta_Novello "Malatesta Novello") (1429), is considered a fine example of a [Renaissance](/wiki/Renaissance "Renaissance") library and holds many valuable manuscripts.

After Novello's death (1465), Cesena returned to the [Papal States](/wiki/Papal_States "Papal States"), but was again seized by a local seignor, [Cesare Borgia](/wiki/Cesare_Borgia "Cesare Borgia"), in 1500. The city was elevated to capital of his powerful though short-lived duchy.

Cesena subsequently turned into a secondary city of the [Papal States](/wiki/Papal_States "Papal States"). In the 18th and 19th centuries [Pope Pius VI](/wiki/Pope_Pius_VI "Pope Pius VI") and [Pope Pius VII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_VII "Pope Pius VII") were born in the city, which also had [Pope Pius VIII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_VIII "Pope Pius VIII") as [bishop](/wiki/Bishop "Bishop"), gaining Cesena the title of "city of the three popes". During the [Napoleonic Wars](/wiki/Napoleonic_Wars "Napoleonic Wars"), it was stripped of numerous monasteries and churches. Some of its citizens had notable roles in the [unification of Italy](/wiki/Unification_of_Italy "Unification of Italy"), in the second half of the 19th century.

During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), Cesena was near the [Gothic Line](/wiki/Gothic_Line "Gothic Line"), which ran along the [Appennini](/wiki/Apennine_Mountains "Apennine Mountains") near the city, and suffered heavily from bombing.

In 1992, it was elevated to the rank of co-capital of the province, together with [Forlì](/wiki/Forl%C3%AC "Forlì").

On July 30, 2015, 1,000 people gathered at the Parco Ippodromo park in Cesena and performed "[Learn to Fly](/wiki/Learn_to_Fly "Learn to Fly")" by the [Foo Fighters](/wiki/Foo_Fighters "Foo Fighters") to convince the band to perform there. The group, later known as the [Rockin' 1000](/wiki/Rockin%27_1000 "Rockin' 1000"), was organized by Foo Fighters fans who played the [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar"), [bass](/wiki/Bass_guitar "Bass guitar"), and [drums](/wiki/Drums "Drums") in unison to a conductor. [Dave Grohl](/wiki/Dave_Grohl "Dave Grohl") responded with a video where he announced in [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") that the band would visit Cesena and perform there. The show was scheduled for November 3, 2015, at the town's indoor sports arena and concert venue, [Carisport](/wiki/Carisport "Carisport"), becoming the kickoff date for their European tour.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Main sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cesena&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Main sights")]

Cesena's monuments include:

* [Abbey of St Maria del Monte](/wiki/Abbey_of_St_Maria_del_Monte "Abbey of St Maria del Monte")
* [Malatestian Fortress](/wiki/Malatestian_Fortress "Malatestian Fortress") (*Rocca Malatestiana*): begun by Cardinal Albornoz in 1380 on the site of an earlier building that hosted [Frederick Barbarossa](/wiki/Frederick_Barbarossa "Frederick Barbarossa") and [Frederick II](/wiki/Frederick_II,_Holy_Roman_Emperor "Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor"). Completed by Papal governor Lorenzo Zane in 1480, it was later used by Cesare Borgia to imprison [Caterina Sforza](/wiki/Caterina_Sforza "Caterina Sforza"). The fortress is octagonal with two main towers: the taller *maschio* ("male") and the shorter *foemina* ("female").
* [Piazza del Popolo](/wiki/Piazza_del_Popolo,_Cesena "Piazza del Popolo, Cesena")
* [Malatestiana Library](/wiki/Malatestiana_Library "Malatestiana Library") — the first public library in [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and a [UNESCO](/wiki/UNESCO "UNESCO") [Memory of the World](/wiki/Memory_of_the_World_Programme "Memory of the World Programme") site.
* [Cathedral of Saint John the Baptist](/wiki/Cesena_Cathedral "Cesena Cathedral")
* [Pinacoteca Comunale](/wiki/Pinacoteca_Comunale_di_Cesena "Pinacoteca Comunale di Cesena"), housed in a former Benedictine monastery.
* [Art collection of Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Cesena](/wiki/Art_collection_of_Fondazione_Cassa_di_Risparmio_di_Cesena "Art collection of Fondazione Cassa di Risparmio di Cesena"), located in the former [Celestine](/wiki/Celestine_order "Celestine order") monastery.
* [Teatro Alessandro Bonci](/wiki/Teatro_Alessandro_Bonci "Teatro Alessandro Bonci")
* [Palazzo Ghini](/wiki/Palazzo_Ghini "Palazzo Ghini")
* Church of [Sant'Agostino](/wiki/Sant%27Agostino,_Cesena "Sant'Agostino, Cesena")
* [Church and Convent dell'Osservanza](/wiki/Chiesa_dell%27Osservanza,_Cesena "Chiesa dell'Osservanza, Cesena")

* [![Piazza del Popolo in Cesena](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/CESENA-3497.jpg/500px-CESENA-3497.jpg)](/wiki/File:CESENA-3497.jpg "Piazza del Popolo in Cesena")

  [Piazza del Popolo](/wiki/Piazza_del_Popolo,_Cesena "Piazza del Popolo, Cesena") in Cesena
* [![The Biblioteca Malatestiana](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Sala_del_Nuti_6.jpg/500px-Sala_del_Nuti_6.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sala_del_Nuti_6.jpg "The Biblioteca Malatestiana")

  The *[Biblioteca Malatestiana](/wiki/Biblioteca_Malatestiana "Biblioteca Malatestiana")*
* [![The towers of the Rocca Malatestiana in Cesena](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/La_rocca_malatestiana_di_Cesena.jpg/500px-La_rocca_malatestiana_di_Cesena.jpg)](/wiki/File:La_rocca_malatestiana_di_Cesena.jpg "The towers of the Rocca Malatestiana in Cesena")

  The towers of the Rocca Malatestiana in Cesena

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cesena&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Economy")]

The main economic sectors in Cesena are:

* [agriculture](/wiki/Agriculture "Agriculture"), in particular fruit and vegetables, and food processing
* [manufacturing](/wiki/Manufacturing "Manufacturing"), in particular mechanics, agricultural and industrial equipment, construction equipment
* in the tertiary sector, [banking](/wiki/Banking "Banking") and [tourism](/wiki/Tourism "Tourism").

Notable companies based in Cesena include:

* [Technogym](/wiki/Technogym "Technogym") (fitness equipment)
* [Soilmec](/wiki/Soilmec "Soilmec") (construction equipment)
* [Olidata](/wiki/Olidata "Olidata") (computers)
* [Cassa di Risparmio di Cesena](/wiki/Cassa_di_Risparmio_di_Cesena "Cassa di Risparmio di Cesena") (banking)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cesena&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transport")]

[Cesena railway station](/wiki/Cesena_railway_station "Cesena railway station"), opened in 1861, forms part of the [Bologna–Ancona railway](/wiki/Bologna%E2%80%93Ancona_railway "Bologna–Ancona railway"). It is situated at Piazza [Giorgio Sanguinetti](/wiki/Giorgio_Sanguinetti "Giorgio Sanguinetti"), to the northeast of the city centre. The nearest airports are [Federico Fellini International Airport](/wiki/Federico_Fellini_International_Airport "Federico Fellini International Airport"), located 39 km (24 mi) south east and [Bologna Guglielmo Marconi Airport](/wiki/Bologna_Guglielmo_Marconi_Airport "Bologna Guglielmo Marconi Airport"), located 94 km (58 mi) north west of Cesena.

## People

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cesena&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: People")]

* [Monty Banks](/wiki/Monty_Banks "Monty Banks") [Mario Bianchi] (1897–1950), Italian-born American actor, comedian and director
* [Christoph Babbi](/wiki/Christoph_Babbi "Christoph Babbi") (1748–1814), violinist, composer, and concertmaster of the Dresden court
* [Gregorio Babbi](/wiki/Gregorio_Babbi "Gregorio Babbi") (1708–1768), operatic tenor
* [Alessandro Bonci](/wiki/Alessandro_Bonci "Alessandro Bonci") (1870–1940), operatic tenor
* [Nicoletta Braschi](/wiki/Nicoletta_Braschi "Nicoletta Braschi") (born 1960), Italian actress, best known for her work with her husband, actor and director [Roberto Benigni](/wiki/Roberto_Benigni "Roberto Benigni")
* [Vincenzo Negrini](/wiki/Vincenzo_Negrini "Vincenzo Negrini") (1804–1840), [bass-baritone](/wiki/Bass-baritone "Bass-baritone") opera singer
* [Marco Pantani](/wiki/Marco_Pantani "Marco Pantani") (1970–2004), road cyclist
* [Michael of Cesena](/wiki/Michael_of_Cesena "Michael of Cesena") (c. 1270–1342), Minister General of the Franciscans from 1316 to his deposition in 1329
* [Pope Pius VI](/wiki/Pope_Pius_VI "Pope Pius VI") (1717–1799), pope from February 15, 1775, to August 29, 1799
* [Pope Pius VII](/wiki/Pope_Pius_VII "Pope Pius VII") (1742–1823), pope from March 14, 1800, to August 20, 1823
* [Scipione Chiaramonti](/wiki/Scipione_Chiaramonti "Scipione Chiaramonti") (1565–1652), philosopher and opponent of Galileo
* [Lorenzo Savadori](/wiki/Lorenzo_Savadori "Lorenzo Savadori") (born 1993), motorcycle racer
* [Obadiah ben Jacob Sforno](/wiki/Obadiah_ben_Jacob_Sforno "Obadiah ben Jacob Sforno"), rabbi
* [Alberto Sughi](/wiki/Alberto_Sughi "Alberto Sughi") (1928–2012), Italian painter
* [Lamberto Tacoli](/wiki/Lamberto_Tacoli "Lamberto Tacoli") (1964), entrepreneur
* [Giuseppe Palmas](/wiki/Giuseppe_Palmas "Giuseppe Palmas") (1918–1977), photographer[[5]](#cite_note-Biblioteca_Malatestiana,_Palmas-5)
* [Carlo Domeniconi](/wiki/Carlo_Domeniconi "Carlo Domeniconi") (born 1947), guitarist and composer
* [Nicky Hayden](/wiki/Nicky_Hayden "Nicky Hayden") (1981–2017), Moto GP and Superbike pilot who died in Cesena on the 22nd March 2017
* [Maurizio Ferrini](/wiki/Maurizio_Ferrini "Maurizio Ferrini") (born 1953), comedian and actor
* [Ubaldo Comandini](/wiki/Ubaldo_Comandini "Ubaldo Comandini") (1869–1925), mayor and cabinet minister
* [Federico Comandini](/wiki/Federico_Comandini "Federico Comandini"), (1893–1967), politician
* [Andrea Navarra](/wiki/Andrea_Navarra "Andrea Navarra") (born 1971), rally driver
